+# AI Dispatch Log
+
+- Timestamp: 2026-05-25T23:52:46.7504508+03:00
+- Dispatch: $Id
+- Issue: #190 - Add label-only dispatch failure taxonomy
+- Issue URL: https://github.com/RustCADs/RGE/issues/190
+- Branch: $Branch
+- Loop exit code: $LoopExit
+- Codex control verdict: $Verdict
+- Loop log: $LoopLog
+
+## Process Trace
+
+1. Queue selected the oldest open ai-dispatch issue.
+2. Queue labelled the issue ai-dispatch-running.
+3. Queue created branch ai-dispatch/ISSUE-190.
+4. Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 ran Codex plan, Claude gate, Claude execute, and Codex control.
+5. Queue wrote this detailed log before staging, committing, merging, or pushing.
+6. If and only if exit code is 0 and Codex control verdict is pass, queue will fast-forward main and push origin/main.
+
+## Files Changed / Added / Deleted
+
+git status --short --untracked-files=all before the queue commit:
+
+~~~text

+?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_EXEC_2026-05-25_23-47-07+0300.md

+?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_TASK_2026-05-25_23-35-52+0300.meta.json
+~~~
+
+git diff --name-status before the queue commit:
+
+~~~text

+~~~
+
+git diff --stat before the queue commit:
+
+~~~text
+Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1 | 71 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++-
+ 1 file changed, 70 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)
+~~~
+
+This log file is also added by the queue before publish:
+
+- ai_dispatch_logs/log_2026-05-25_23-52-46+0300.md
+
+## Generated Run Files
+
+- .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-190/claude.ready.stderr.txt (0 bytes)
+- .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-190/claude.ready.envelope.json (2770 bytes)

+- .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-190/claude.execute.round0.stderr.txt (0 bytes)
+- .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-190/claude.execute.round0.envelope.json (5050 bytes)
+- .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-190/claude.execute.round0.md (1125 bytes)
+- .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-190/verification.round0.log (1081558 bytes)
+- .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-190/codex.prompt.md (1050 bytes)
+- .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-190/codex.control.round0.json (3000 bytes)
+- .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-190/codex.control.round0.log (1546748 bytes)
+
+## Claude Marker Summary
+
+### .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-190/claude.plan_gate.rev0.md

+GATE_VERDICT: approve
+```
+
+## Codex Control JSON
+
+~~~json
+{"verdict":"pass","summary":"No blocking findings. The change is scoped to Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1, adds the requested terminal failure taxonomy labels and applies/verifies them only for non-retry terminal failures. Retry, success, selection, publish, and branch paths are otherwise unchanged. The orchestrator verification log reports VERIFY OK for all 7 canonical steps.","task_packet":"ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_TASK_2026-05-25_23-35-52+0300.md","exec_packet":"ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_EXEC_2026-05-25_23-47-07+0300.md","changed_files":["Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1","ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_TASK_2026-05-25_23-35-52+0300.md","ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_TASK_2026-05-25_23-35-52+0300.meta.json","ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_EXEC_2026-05-25_23-47-07+0300.md","ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_EXEC_2026-05-25_23-47-07+0300.meta.json"],"required_fixes":[],"verification":[{"command":"git status --short --branch --untracked-files=all","result":"On branch ai-dispatch/ISSUE-190. One tracked source modification: Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1. Untracked files are limited to this dispatch's TASK/EXEC handoff packets and sidecars.","exit_code":0},{"command":"git diff -- Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1","result":"Reviewed diff: 70 insertions/1 deletion. Adds Get-FailureTaxonomyLabels, idempotent failure labels, terminal-failure taxonomy relabeling, and terminal-failure label verification only.","exit_code":0},{"command":"git diff --check","result":"No whitespace or conflict-marker errors.","exit_code":0},{"command":"Get-Content -LiteralPath .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-190/verification.round0.log -Tail 20","result":"Canonical dispatch verification completed with: VERIFY OK: all 7 verification step(s) passed.","exit_code":0},{"command":"Static inspection of Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1","result":"Success path receives no taxonomy labels; retry path receives no taxonomy labels; terminal failed path adds ai-dispatch-failed plus selected ai-dispatch-failure-* label(s), and finalization verifies those labels only when runFailed is true and willRetry is false.","exit_code":0}],"commit_readiness":"ready_for_publish","commands_run":["git status --short --branch","git diff --stat","Get-Content -Raw AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md","Get-Content -Raw ai_handoffs/AI_HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md","Get-Content -Raw ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_TASK_2026-05-25_23-35-52+0300.md","Get-Content -Raw ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_EXEC_2026-05-25_23-47-07+0300.md","Get-Content -Raw .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-190/verification.round0.log","git diff --name-only","git diff -- Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1","git diff --check","Get-Content -LiteralPath .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-190/verification.round0.log -Tail 20","findstr /n /i /c:\"Verification gate\" Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1","findstr /n /i /c:\"Codex requested\" Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1","findstr /n /i /c:\"Codex control\" Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1","findstr /n /i /c:\"MaxCorrectionRounds\" Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1","findstr /n /i /c:\"stalled\" Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1","findstr /n /i /c:\"timeout\" Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1"]}
+~~~

@@ -0,0 +1,138 @@
+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-190
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-25_23-47-07+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1 — added Get-FailureTaxonomyLabels helper; created seven `ai-dispatch-failure-*` labels idempotently; applied taxonomy label(s) to terminal-failure relabel; extended label finalization verification to require taxonomy labels on terminal failed issues
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_TASK_2026-05-25_23-35-52+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`:
+  - Added a new helper `Get-FailureTaxonomyLabels` immediately before the
+    `# --- Environment ---` section. It is a pure text classifier over the
+    queue-local outcomes `$LoopText`, `$ExecStatus`, and `$PublishHardFailed`;
+    it does not read or write files, call git/gh, or alter loop output. It
+    always returns at least one taxonomy label (defaulting to
+    `ai-dispatch-failure-unknown`).
+  - Extended the existing idempotent `$labelSpec` list inside the bookkeeping
+    label setup block to also create the seven new failure-class labels
+    (`stall`, `timeout`, `blocked`, `verification`, `control`, `publish`,
+    `unknown`) through the same `gh label create ... --force` path that
+    creates the existing queue labels.
+  - After `$willRetry` is computed in the post-publish finalization block,
+    added a `$taxonomyLabels` computation guarded by
+    `$runFailed -and -not $willRetry`, so taxonomy is only computed for
+    terminal failures.
+  - Extended the non-retry relabel command builder so it appends one
+    `--add-label <taxonomy>` argument per selected taxonomy label. The retry
+    relabel branch and the success path are untouched.
+  - Extended the post-relabel label finalization verification: only when
+    `$runFailed` is true, every selected taxonomy label must also be present
+    on the issue. A missing taxonomy label flips `$labelOk` to `$false`,
+    which causes the existing `Fail` path to exit non-zero — matching the
+    existing `ai-dispatch-failed` finalization behavior.
+
+### Tests
+
+- None added. The task is label-only and explicitly forbids adding tests,
+  dependencies, scripts, schemas, docs, workflows, Rust changes, Cargo
+  changes, or generated fixtures.
+
+### Docs
+
+- None added.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+`Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` is the only source file edited. All other
+changes are this dispatch's own allowed `ai_handoffs/` artifacts.
+
+## Verification Results
+
+- PowerShell parser validation of `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`
+  (via `[System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseFile`) → exit 0
+  (`parser OK`, zero diagnostic errors).
+- `git diff --check` → exit 0 (no whitespace / conflict markers).
+- `powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1`
+  → exit 0; final line `VERIFY OK: all 7 verification step(s) passed.`
+- Static inspection of the final diff:
+  - Successful runs (`-not $runFailed`) still go through the existing
+    done path at line ~1473 and receive no `ai-dispatch-failure-*` label
+    (`$taxonomyLabels` is `@()` whenever `$runFailed` is false, and the new
+    `foreach ($tl in $taxonomyLabels)` block is a no-op for an empty list).
+  - `$willRetry` issues still go through the existing retry-only relabel
+    that only removes `$runLabel` and adds `$retryLabel`; the taxonomy
+    `foreach` is only inside the non-retry `else` branch.
+  - Terminal failed issues (`$runFailed -and -not $willRetry`) now have
+    `--add-label $failLabel` plus one `--add-label <taxonomy>` per selected
+    taxonomy label, and the label finalization verification block requires
+    each `$taxonomyLabels` entry to be present whenever `$runFailed` is
+    true. Taxonomy is not required for successful or retry-queued issues.
+- `git diff --name-only` → `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`. Untracked items
+  (`git status --short`) are limited to this dispatch's own
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_TASK_*.md` and `.meta.json` (created by the
+  Planner stage and tracked here) and this `ISSUE-190_EXEC_*` packet plus
+  its sidecar when finalized. No `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` was created by
+  this run (no live dispatch loop executed).
+
+## Deviations from Task Packet
+
+None — execution stayed strictly within the Task Packet scope. The only
+source edit is `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`, no retry / publish / branch /
+selection / cap / watchdog / verification / schema behavior was changed,
+and no files outside the allowed surface were touched.
+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+- Verification timeouts: the classification order in the task (publish ->
+  blocked -> stall -> timeout -> verification -> control -> unknown) means
+  a `Verification timed out` loop-text line is classified as
+  `ai-dispatch-failure-timeout` rather than `ai-dispatch-failure-verification`,
+  because the timeout rule fires before the verification rule. This matches
+  the literal ordering in the TASK packet and the acceptance criterion
+  "Generic timeouts that are not Codex watchdog stalls are classified as
+  `ai-dispatch-failure-timeout`." Flagging it explicitly so the reviewer
+  can confirm the intent.
+- The `(?i)maxcorrectionrounds=\d+ is exhausted` clause in the control rule
+  is intentionally only reached after the verification rule, so the
+  Verification gate's `"Verification gate failed (exit X) and
+  MaxCorrectionRounds=N is exhausted."` line classifies as
+  `verification`, while the control gate's `"Codex requested changes, but
+  MaxCorrectionRounds=N is exhausted."` line classifies as `control`. Both
+  fall through to the more specific anchor (`verification gate failed` and
+  `codex requested changes`) first; the `maxcorrectionrounds=...exhausted`
+  clause is a defense-in-depth catch for log truncation. Confirming the
+  reviewer is happy with this layering.
+
+## Worktree State
+
+- Tracked files: `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` (modified, uncommitted per protocol).
+- Untracked items:
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_TASK_2026-05-25_23-35-52+0300.md`
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_TASK_2026-05-25_23-35-52+0300.meta.json`
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_EXEC_2026-05-25_23-47-07+0300.md` (this packet)
+  - `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_EXEC_2026-05-25_23-47-07+0300.meta.json` (added at finalize)
+- Branch: `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-190`
+- Last commit: `0a61780` chore(dispatch): queue failure taxonomy task
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-190
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+NEXT_ROLE: REVIEWER_AI
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
+---

@@ -0,0 +1,316 @@
+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-190
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-25_23-35-52+0300
+REVISION: 0
+RELATED_FILES:
+- Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_TASK_2026-05-25_23-35-52+0300.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_TASK_2026-05-25_23-35-52+0300.meta.json
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_EXEC_*.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_EXEC_*.meta.json
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_CORRECT_*.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_CORRECT_*.meta.json
+- ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md
+- GitHub issue #190
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Add a label-only terminal failure taxonomy to `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` for
+GitHub issue #190. The queue already halts autonomous progress by marking
+terminal failures with `ai-dispatch-failed`; this dispatch must preserve that
+halt behavior while adding durable, more specific failure-class labels for
+human triage and later policy analysis. The change must not alter retry
+eligibility, recovery routes, issue selection, cap counting, publishing, branch
+policy, or any dispatch loop behavior.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`
+- `Register-AiDispatchSchedule.ps1`
+- `Get-AiDispatchHealth.ps1`
+- `Watch-AiDispatch.ps1`
+- `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1`
+- `new-handoff.ps1`
+- `.ai/**`, including `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`,
+  `.ai/codex_control.schema.json`, `.ai/handoff.schema.json`,
+  `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`, control-review schemas, preflight artifacts,
+  watchdog implementation, and JSONL trace schema or path policy
+- `ai_handoffs/**`, except this dispatch's own `ISSUE-190_TASK`,
+  `ISSUE-190_EXEC`, and `ISSUE-190_CORRECT` Markdown packets and matching
+  `.meta.json` sidecars
+- `ai_dispatch_logs/**`, except this dispatch's own exact queue log artifact
+  if the queue runner creates one
+- Rust source, Cargo manifests, `Cargo.lock`, tests, fixtures, workflows,
+  docs, dashboards, status files, architecture-lint files, `.gitignore`,
+  repository metadata, sandbox worktrees, or any file outside the allowed
+  source/artifact surface
+
+### MAY add new files
+
+- This dispatch's own generated handoff artifacts only:
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_TASK_2026-05-25_23-35-52+0300.meta.json`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_EXEC_*.md`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_EXEC_*.meta.json`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_CORRECT_*.md`, and
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-190_CORRECT_*.meta.json`.
+- This dispatch's own exact `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` artifact if the queue
+  runner creates one. Do not manually create or edit unrelated dispatch logs.
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+
+- Any source file, test file, fixture, generated asset, manifest, lockfile,
+  dependency file, crate, workflow, schema, script, doc, ADR, plan, status
+  file, architecture-lint artifact, dashboard, `.gitignore` entry, or
+  repository metadata file.
+- Any handoff packet or sidecar for another dispatch.
+- Any `REVIEW` or `CLOSEOUT` packet for this orchestrator dispatch.
+- Any `ai_dispatch_logs/**` file other than this dispatch's own exact queue log
+  artifact if one is created by the queue runner.
+- Any durable JSONL trace file, dashboard, aggregation artifact, or local
+  runtime artifact intended for commit.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- Add idempotent creation for these taxonomy labels in the existing queue
+  label setup path that already creates `ai-dispatch`, `ai-dispatch-running`,
+  `ai-dispatch-done`, `ai-dispatch-failed`, and `ai-dispatch-retry`:
+  - `ai-dispatch-failure-stall`
+  - `ai-dispatch-failure-timeout`
+  - `ai-dispatch-failure-blocked`
+  - `ai-dispatch-failure-verification`
+  - `ai-dispatch-failure-control`
+  - `ai-dispatch-failure-publish`
+  - `ai-dispatch-failure-unknown`
+- Add a compact queue-local helper that classifies terminal failed runs using
+  only data already available after the dispatch loop and publish decision:
+  `$loopExit`, `$loopText`, `$verdict`, `$execStatus`, `$publishFailed`,
+  `$publishHardFailed`, and nearby queue-local state already computed in
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`.
+- The helper must produce at least one selected taxonomy label for every
+  terminal failed run. A single primary label is sufficient and preferred.
+  If no more specific class matches, select
+  `ai-dispatch-failure-unknown`.
+- Classification order must preserve the intended signal:
+  1. Publish failure: select `ai-dispatch-failure-publish` for publish-pipeline
+     failures.
+  2. Blocked execution: select `ai-dispatch-failure-blocked` when
+     `$execStatus -eq 'blocked'`.
+  3. Codex watchdog stall: select `ai-dispatch-failure-stall` when loop text
+     contains Codex watchdog stall wording, including `codex exec stalled` or
+     `no log growth`.
+  4. Generic timeout: select `ai-dispatch-failure-timeout` when loop text
+     contains timeout wording and the stall class did not match.
+  5. Verification failure: select `ai-dispatch-failure-verification` when loop
+     text contains verification-gate failure wording.
+  6. Codex control failure: select `ai-dispatch-failure-control` when loop
+     text contains control-block or exhausted-control-change wording.
+  7. Unknown: select `ai-dispatch-failure-unknown` otherwise.
+- Apply taxonomy labels only when `$runFailed` is true and `$willRetry` is
+  false. This includes failed retry attempts, blocked executions, and terminal
+  publish failures, but excludes successful runs and first-attempt failures
+  that are being re-queued for automatic retry.
+- Preserve the existing `ai-dispatch-failed` label behavior unchanged:
+  terminal failed issues must still receive `ai-dispatch-failed`; retry-queued
+  failures must still avoid `ai-dispatch-failed`; successful issues must still
+  avoid `ai-dispatch-failed`.
+- Add the selected taxonomy label or labels to the existing terminal failure
+  relabel command without adding any taxonomy label to the retry relabel path
+  or the success relabel path.
+- Extend label finalization verification so terminal failed issues require the
+  selected taxonomy label or labels in addition to the existing expected
+  terminal labels. A terminal failed issue that lacks its selected taxonomy
+  label must make label finalization fail non-zero just like a missing
+  `ai-dispatch-failed` label does today.
+- Do not make taxonomy labels participate in issue selection, cap counting,
+  halt checks, retry eligibility, branch archival, orphan recovery, publish
+  eligibility, branch deletion, cleanup, or autonomous task selection.
+- It is acceptable, but not required, to mention the selected taxonomy label or
+  labels in the result comment for human readability. The durable output is the
+  GitHub label set.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- The only source file changed is `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`.
+- The existing queue labels are still created and used with their existing
+  names: `ai-dispatch`, `ai-dispatch-running`, `ai-dispatch-done`,
+  `ai-dispatch-failed`, and `ai-dispatch-retry`.
+- The seven new taxonomy labels are created idempotently through the same
+  `gh label create ... --force` setup style as the existing queue labels.
+- Successful runs retain the existing successful label outcome: the issue has
+  `ai-dispatch-done`, does not have `ai-dispatch-running`, does not have
+  `ai-dispatch`, does not have `ai-dispatch-retry`, does not have
+  `ai-dispatch-failed`, and receives no `ai-dispatch-failure-*` taxonomy label.
+- Successful control-passed publish behavior is unchanged: publish eligibility,
+  fast-forward merge, push, branch deletion, issue close behavior, and
+  successful result comments are not changed except for unavoidable surrounding
+  code movement if needed for the taxonomy helper.
+- First-attempt retry-eligible failures retain the existing retry outcome:
+  `$willRetry` stays true under the same conditions as before, the issue keeps
+  `ai-dispatch`, receives `ai-dispatch-retry`, does not receive
+  `ai-dispatch-failed`, and receives no `ai-dispatch-failure-*` taxonomy label.
+- Terminal failed runs are exactly the cases where `$runFailed` is true and
+  `$willRetry` is false. Those issues receive `ai-dispatch-done`,
+  `ai-dispatch-failed`, and at least one selected `ai-dispatch-failure-*`
+  taxonomy label, while losing `ai-dispatch-running`, `ai-dispatch`, and
+  `ai-dispatch-retry`.
+- Codex watchdog stalls are classified as `ai-dispatch-failure-stall`, not as
+  `ai-dispatch-failure-timeout`, when loop text contains `codex exec stalled`
+  or `no log growth`.
+- Generic timeouts that are not Codex watchdog stalls are classified as
+  `ai-dispatch-failure-timeout`.
+- Blocked executions with `$execStatus -eq 'blocked'` are classified as
+  `ai-dispatch-failure-blocked` and remain terminal, not retryable.
+- Verification-gate failures and exhausted verification correction rounds are
+  classified as `ai-dispatch-failure-verification` when their loop text carries
+  the existing verification failure wording.
+- Codex control blocks and exhausted control correction rounds are classified
+  as `ai-dispatch-failure-control` when their loop text carries the existing
+  control failure wording.
+- Publish-pipeline terminal failures are classified as
+  `ai-dispatch-failure-publish`.
+- Any terminal failed run that does not match a more specific class receives
+  `ai-dispatch-failure-unknown`.
+- Label finalization verification checks for taxonomy labels only on terminal
+  failed issues. It does not require taxonomy labels for successful issues or
+  retry-queued issues.
+- No retry eligibility, same-phase retry behavior, recovery route, queue
+  selection filter, cap-counting behavior, publish behavior, branch policy,
+  Auto Loop behavior, watchdog behavior, preflight audit behavior, JSONL trace
+  schema/path behavior, Rust/Cargo behavior, docs, dashboards, workflows, or
+  schemas change.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- This dispatch is label-only. It must not add new retry routes, same-phase
+  retries, recovery automation, alerting, aggregation, dashboards, health
+  summaries, task selection changes, or policy changes.
+- Do not edit `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`; do not change the Codex watchdog,
+  native command timeout handling, preflight audit, prompt text, marker
+  parsing, control-review schema, or verification gate behavior.
+- Do not edit `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`; do not change autonomous queue
+  selection, issue filing, cap counting, halt behavior, scheduler behavior, or
+  JSONL trace schema.
+- Do not change how `$runFailed`, `$willRetry`, `$runBlocked`, `$isRetry`,
+  `$publishFailed`, or `$publishHardFailed` determine retry eligibility or
+  terminal state. The taxonomy must consume those outcomes, not redefine them.
+- Do not add taxonomy labels to issue list queries, pending issue filtering,
+  orphan recovery decisions, branch archival decisions, publish eligibility, or
+  cap logic.
+- Do not add tests, dependencies, scripts, schemas, docs, workflows, Rust

+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt without committing and request fresh guidance if any of
+the following occur:
+
+- The taxonomy cannot be implemented by editing only
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` plus this dispatch's own generated
+  `ai_handoffs` and `ai_dispatch_logs` artifacts.
+- Adding taxonomy labels would require editing `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`,

+- The implementation would change retry eligibility, same-phase retry,
+  recovery routes, queue selection, cap counting, `ai-dispatch-failed` halt
+  behavior, publish behavior, branch policy, branch archival, orphan recovery,
+  Auto Loop behavior, JSONL trace schema/path policy, Rust/Cargo behavior,

+## Planner Notes
+
+Use the post-loop/post-publish finalization block in `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`
+as the integration point. The existing variables `$runFailed`, `$runBlocked`,
+and `$willRetry` already define whether a run is successful, retry-queued, or
+terminal. Compute taxonomy labels after those values are known, and add them
+only to the non-retry terminal failure relabel and verification path.
+
+Prefer a small helper with case-insensitive text matching over new state,
+schema, trace output, or issue queries. The classification helper should not
+alter the loop output, read or write extra files, call GitHub, or affect
+control flow. It should return `ai-dispatch-failure-unknown` if no specific
+rule matches.

+MUST NOT change retry eligibility same-phase retry recovery routes queue selection cap counting publish behavior branch policy Auto Loop watchdog preflight JSONL schema Rust Cargo docs or dashboards
+MUST preserve successful issue labels and successful publish behavior unchanged
+MUST extend label finalization verification to require taxonomy labels on terminal failed issues
+MUST run PowerShell parser validation for Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1, git diff --check, and the canonical .ai/dispatch.verify.ps1 gate successfully
+```
